title: Distillation objectives: soft labels and rationales
description: How matching teacher logits, intermediate states, or generated rationales transfers capability to a smaller student.
body: Use when planning what signal a student should learn from a teacher. Distillation can match the teacher's soft-label distribution (temperature-scaled logits), align intermediate representations, or train on teacher-generated rationales and answers. Do not assume hard-label imitation captures reasoning — soft targets and rationales transfer more of the teacher's behavior. Actions: pick the objective the student capacity can absorb, train, and measure the task gap to the teacher. Validation signals include a student-teacher benchmark and an objective ablation showing the chosen signal helps. Risk boundary: a student too small cannot absorb the teacher; if the gap stays large, escalate to a bigger student rather than ship.
